nameMeanings.name: Lacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Lacon in Wem (Shrops). This name has probably been confused with or absorbed by   Lakin   (1), especially in counties adjoining Shrops, such as Staffs.

2 English: while the name in Yorks could be that in (1) through migration, its appearance in late 14th-century Clint in the north-west of WR Yorks, and the persistent final -can in most early forms of the surname, suggest that it may have had a different but unknown origin.

variants.name: Laken
meanings: 1  English:  see  Lakin  .
2  English:  see  Lacon  .

variants.name: Laking
meanings: English:  see  Lacon   and   Lakin  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

nameMeanings.name: serra
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Eduardo, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Enrique, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Enrico, Guido, Salvatore, Amedeo, Elio, Marco, Nicolo, Sandro, Caesar, Carlo.  Portuguese Henrique, Joaquim.

Italian, Portuguese, and Catalan: topographic name from Italian, Portuguese, Catalan serra ‘ridge or chain of hills; mountain range’ (from Latin serra ‘saw’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, in Italy e.g. Serra d'Aniello and Serra Pedace (Cosenza), Serra San Bruno (Vibo Valentia), and Serracapriola (Foggia), in Valencia and Catalonia (Spain) e.g. Serra d'Almos or Serra d'en Galceran.
Catalan (Serrà): topographic name for someone who lived by a sierra, from Catalan serrà, an adjective derived from serra (see 1 above), the same as Castilian Serrano .

variants.name: Serritella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amelio, Angelo, Carmela, Rocco, Romeo, Santa.
Italian: possibly a topographic name related to   Serra  .

variants.name: Sera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Armando, Fernando, Jose, Alberto, Alvaro, Ana, Assunta, Elia, Enrique, Ernesto, Evelio.  Japanese Yoshio, Hideo, Hitomi, Katsumi, Kenji, Kimihiro, Masao, Mikio, Nobuhiro, Sadao, Takeo, Tsuneo.
Portuguese, Galician, and Catalan: most probably a variant of   Serra  .
Italian (Séra): from a short form of a personal name with this ending, such as Baldissera, Bonasera or Serafino .

variants.name: Serret
meanings: Catalan and southern French: topographic name from a diminutive of Catalan   Serra   and Occitan   Serre  .

variants.name: Serrano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Carlos, Miguel, Francisco, Jesus, Jorge, Ricardo, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Wenseslao.

Spanish: topographic name for someone who lived by a mountain ridge or chain of hills, from an adjectival derivative of serra (see Serra ). This surname is also found in the Philippines, Portugal, and Brazil.
Italian (in Calabria Serranò): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Serra (see Serra ). The surname Serrano may also be of Spanish origin.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sinispella de Lacon serra was born in 1150, in Laconi, Oristano, Sardinia, Italy, her father, Barison I de Lacon Serra, was 27 and her mother, Pelegrina di Arborea, was 23. She had at least 1 daughter with Guillaume de Sabran. She died in 1204, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sinispella de Lacon serra est née en 1150, à Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Barison I de Lacon Serra, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Pelegrina di Arborea, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Guillaume de Sabran. Elle est décédée en 1204, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sinispella de Lacon serra 1150 in Laconi, Oristano, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Barison I de Lacon Serra 27 und ihre Mutter Pelegrina di Arborea 23. Sie hatte mit Guillaume de Sabran mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1204 im Alter von 54 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sinispella de Lacon serra è nata il nel 1150 nel Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, sua padre, Barison I de Lacon Serra, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Pelegrina di Arborea, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Guillaume de Sabran. Morì nel 1204, all'età di 54 anni.

イタリアサルデグナオリスタノラコニ
ja.Mévouillon, Drôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes,: フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスドルoメムeボウイロン
ja.lifeSummary: Sinispella de Lacon serraが1150年にイタリアサルデグナオリスタノラコニで生まれた時，父Barison I de Lacon Serraは27歳，母Pelegrina di Arboreaは23歳であった。彼女はGuillaume de Sabranとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1204年に54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sinispella de Lacon serra(이)가 1150년 이탈리아 사르데그나 오리스탠오 라코니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Barison I de Lacon Serra(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Pelegrina di Arborea(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Guillaume de Sabran(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sinispella de Lacon serra nasceu em 1150, em Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Barison I de Lacon Serra, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Pelegrina di Arborea, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Guillaume de Sabran. Ela faleceu em 1204, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sinispella de Lacon serra родилась в 1150 г. в местности лакони, ористано, сардегна, Италия, её отцу по имени Barison I de Lacon Serra было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Pelegrina di Arborea, 23. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Guillaume de Sabran. Она умерла в 1204 г. в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sinispella de Lacon serra nació en 1150, en Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, su papá, Barison I de Lacon Serra, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Pelegrina di Arborea, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Guillaume de Sabran. Murió en 1204, a la edad de 54 años.
